# DreamFi

DreamFi is an internal product team OS built on top of Onyx. It gives product, risk, and operations teams a shared workspace for asking grounded questions, reviewing evidence across connected systems, generating reusable artifacts, and enforcing trust gates before work is promoted or published.

## Primary docs

- `README.md` - product overview, local setup, repo layout, deployment notes, and API summary.
- `docs/architecture/onyx-integration.md` - how DreamFi talks to Onyx, which endpoints are used, and how citations/documents flow into confidence scoring.
- `docs/adr/009-onyx-substrate.md` - architectural decision record for treating Onyx as the retrieval and persona substrate.
- `docs/adr/010-dreamfi-db-minimal.md` - architectural decision record for the minimal DreamFi database model.
- `AGENTS.md` - repository rules for agents and contributors.

## Product surfaces

- `Ask` - start from a question, retrieve evidence, and keep receipts attached to answers.
- `Topic rooms` - recurring decision spaces such as onboarding, KYC conversion, funding, and lifecycle messaging.
- `Source workspaces` - product-facing views over systems like Jira, Confluence, Dragonboat, Metabase, PostHog, Klaviyo, NetXD, Sardine, Socure, and Google Analytics.
- `Generated artifacts` - weekly PM briefs, technical PRDs, business PRDs, risk BRDs, and similar outputs.
- `Trust review` - blocked work, risky work, publish readiness, and system-health visibility.

## Architecture

- Backend: `dreamfi/`
- Frontend source: `generators/web/src/`
- Frontend build output served by the API: `generators/web/dist/`
- Locked eval templates and runners: `evals/`
- Tests: `tests/`

DreamFi uses a governed skill layer underneath the frontend. Prompt templates are rendered from active prompt versions, sent through `dreamfi.onyx.client.OnyxClient`, evaluated by locked runners, scored for confidence/export readiness, and then surfaced for review or publish.

## Key backend routes

- `GET /ready` - deploy liveness check.
- `GET /health` - service and Onyx reachability.
- `GET /api/console` - JSON payload for the product console.
- `GET /console` - React console UI.
- `GET /v1/skills/{skill_id}/history` - recent eval rounds.
- `POST /v1/skills/{skill_id}/eval-round` - run an eval round.
- `POST /v1/skills/{skill_id}/promote` - activate a prompt version when rules pass.
- `POST /v1/skills/{skill_id}/publish` - record and enforce publish policy.

## Development commands

- `make bootstrap` - install/start local dependencies and DreamFi.
- `make dreamfi-up` - start the DreamFi stack when Onyx already exists.
- `make seed` - seed skills and Onyx personas/document sets.
- `make test` - local unit and mocked integration coverage.
- `make test-live` - live Onyx tests only.
- `make lint` - Ruff checks.
- `make format` - Ruff formatting.

## Contributor notes for language models

- Do not modify locked eval templates or runners under `evals/`.
- Do not hand-edit shipped Alembic revisions; create new migrations instead.
- All Onyx HTTP must go through `dreamfi.onyx.client.OnyxClient`.
- Unit tests should mock Onyx with `respx`; avoid network calls in unit tests.
- The frontend served in production comes from checked-in assets under `generators/web/dist/`, so rebuild the web app after frontend changes that affect shipped UI.
